data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-5028c8e6">
    <title>visuals: SVG/Mermaid diagrams + nano-banana hero graphics for microsite</title>
    <description>
V3 of 3 children under epic ddx-53c96967. Depends on V1 (ddx-c5dce902); benefits from but does not block on V2.

Generate two classes of visuals:

A) TECHNICAL DIAGRAMS (SVG or Mermaid, committed alongside docs):
- Three-layer stack: DDx (platform) ↔ HELIX (workflow) ↔ Dun (quality). Show how a workflow tool consumes DDx primitives.
- Bead lifecycle: open → ready → in_progress → closed (with review, push-conflict, declined branches).
- Execute-loop flow: claim bead → render prompt → run agent → review → merge/push (auto-recover on race) → close.
- Persona/role binding: roles in workflow (e.g. code-reviewer) → persona definitions in library → project binding in .ddx/config.yaml.
- Project-local install model: <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/, <projectRoot>/.agents/skills/, <projectRoot>/.claude/skills/ all real-files; ddx-server is the only global artifact.

Place SVGs at website/static/diagrams/<name>.svg or use Mermaid code blocks inside markdown. Wire them into the microsite concept pages where V2 left placeholders.

B) HERO GRAPHIC (raster, polished, for the microsite landing page):
- Use the nano-banana-pro-openrouter skill to generate a hero image.
- Subject: visual metaphor for document-driven development with AI agents. Suggest: a structured document tree feeding into agent workflows, not generic 'AI' clip art. The user explicitly wants something distinctive, not generic AI aesthetic.
- 2K output minimum. Multiple variants OK; pick the strongest.
- Place at website/static/hero/landing.png (or .webp for size).
- Wire into website/content/_index.md or wherever the landing hero is rendered.

PREREQUISITES:
- Set OPENROUTER_API_KEY in env or .env to use the nano-banana skill.
- If the API key isn't available, this bead creates the SVG diagrams and prepares the prompt + integration point for the hero, then declines the hero portion with a clear blocker note.

OUT OF SCOPE: animations, video, anything interactive. Static SVG/PNG only.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. SVGs (or Mermaid) for: three-layer-stack, bead-lifecycle, execute-loop, persona-binding, project-local-install. Committed under website/static/diagrams/ or as Mermaid in markdown. 2. Diagram placeholders left by V2 are filled in. 3. Hero graphic generated and committed at website/static/hero/landing.* (PNG or WebP), wired into the landing page _index.md. OR: if OPENROUTER_API_KEY unavailable, this AC is replaced with a documented blocker note in the bead's notes field and a prompt+integration-point ready for follow-up. 4. Hugo build succeeds (or theme check passes) with the new assets. 5. No generic AI clip art — the hero is distinctive and on-message for document-driven development.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
AC#3 blocker: OPENROUTER_API_KEY unavailable + nano-banana-pro-openrouter skill not installed at execution time. SVG diagrams (AC#1, #2) committed under website/static/diagrams/. Hero portion declined per AC#3 contingency. Prompt + integration point ready: see website/static/hero/HERO_BLOCKER.md (uncomment HERO_GRAPHIC_PLACEHOLDER block in website/content/_index.md once landing.webp/.png is generated). Hugo build passes (140 pages, 30 static files, 0 errors).
    </notes>
    <labels>kind:docs, area:docs, area:website, kind:design</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-53c96967" base-rev="2ffe0503ea5c28d8a980e22636a68479d903a670" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260429T164209-2e5c0db6"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260429T164209-2e5c0db6/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed `36bac94e`. AC walkthrough:

1. **Diagrams for the 5 named topics** — Mermaid blocks for `three-layer-stack`, `bead-lifecycle`, `execute-loop`, `persona-binding`, `project-local-install` (plus a bonus `documents-drive-agents`) in `concepts/_index.md` and `concepts/architecture.md`.
2. **V2 placeholders filled** — all six `<!-- diagram: ... -->` markers replaced.
3. **Hero (AC#3 contingency)** — `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` unset and skill not installed; deferred per the bead's contingency. `website/static/hero/HERO_BLOCKER.md` carries the prompt + integration steps; `_index.md` has a `HERO_GRAPHIC_PLACEHOLDER` block ready to uncomment.
4. **Hugo build** — `hugo --minify` exits 0 (143 pages, 0 errors); Mermaid present in rendered HTML.
5. **No generic AI clip art** — none shipped; the deferred prompt explicitly excludes it.
